<DESC>This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for a service/supply contract for commercial items in accordance with the format in Subpart 12.6, as supplemented with the additional information included in this notice. THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONSTITUTES THE ONLY SOLICITTION; PROPOSALS ARE BEING REQUESTED AND A WRITTEN SOLICITATION WILL NOT BE ISSUED. The solicitation and incorporated provisions and clauses are those in effect through Federal Acquisition Circular 97-23 effective February 20, 2001The solicitation number is MT582-01 and this is a Request for Proposal. This acquisition is unrestricted. The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code is 56199?Scrap Metal Services. Proposals are due in this office no later than Thursday, October 11, 2001 at 2:00PM ET. Federal Prison Industries Inc. intends to enter into a firm fixed price requirements type contract for a period of performance of five years with a five years option, if exercised. Place of Performance: UNICOR El Reno, Oklahoma. UNICOR intends to award on an ?All or None Basis?. The quantities specified in the solicitation are estimates only; and shall not obligate the Government. Orders may be issued from date of contract award through five years thereafter and an additional 5 years if the option is exercised. The contractor must provide all manpower and equipment necessary for the loading, removal, and replacing of the bins for all three items: steel scrap, estimated quantity of 75 ton; tin cans, estimated quantity of 75

1107 19 Department of Veterans Affairs 263 Network Contracting Office 23 (NCO 23) Department of Veterans Affairs Medical and Regional Office Center 57105 H 561621 Department of Veterans Affairs;NETWORK 23 CONTRACTING OFFICE;2501 W. 22nd St.;Sioux Falls SD 57105 H--Fire Alarm Inspection, Testing, and Repair Service 36C26320Q0037 111519 01142020 John Becker john.becker3@va.gov John Becker, Contracting Officer Page 2 of 2 NOTICE OF INTENT TO AWARD SOLE SOURCE This is a Notice of Intent published in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) 5.101(a)(1) requiring the dissemination of information regarding proposed contract actions. This Notice of Intent is for a proposed award of a sole source, firm-fixed price contract under the authority of FAR 8.405-6(a)(1)(i)(B); only one source is capable of providing the supplies or services required at the level of quality required because the supplies or services are unique or highly specialized. The Department of Veterans Affairs, Network Contracting Office 23 intends to award a sole source, firm-fixed price contract to Johnson Controls Fire Protection LP to provide Fire Alarm Inspection, Testing, and Repair Services to support the Central Iowa VA Health Care System, Des Moines, Iowa. Johnson Controls Fire Protection LP is the only known firm having unfettered access and software codes to access simplex fire alarm panels to conduct fire alarm inspection and testing, and repairs. Proprietary software and computer hardware are currently being utilized on the Simplex fire alarm, fire doors, and sprinkler system. The hardware includes circuit boards and partitioning circuit boards within the Simplex fire alarm panels. The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code for this requirement is 561621, Security Systems Services (except Locks

1106 19 Department of Agriculture Agricultural Research Service Southeast Area J 811310 NOTICE OF INTENT TO SOLE SOURCE MAINTENANCE CONTRACT FOR YORK CHILLERS 12405B20Q0003 111219 Shalunda S. Mix, Contract Specialist, Phone 662-686-5346, Fax 662-686-5373, Email shalunda.mix@ars.usda.gov Notice of Intent: The USDA, ARS, SEA intends to award a sole source procurement to Johnson Controls, Inc. P. O. Box 2012, Milwaukee, WI 53201-2012, as they are the sole source for this type of service pursuant to the authority of 41 U.S.C. 253(c)(l ) and FAR 6.302-1, Only One Responsible Source and No Other Supplies or Services Will Satisfy Agency Requirements. The procurement is for an annual service contract for the repair, service and maintenance of the York Chillers and Controls at Bldg. 2, JWDSRC in Stoneville, MS. Johnson Controls will provide the service for the (2) York 500 Ton Chillers and the METASYS BAS Controls at the USDA-ARS, Stoneville, MS for a one-year period. The systems that Johnson Controls will maintain are the York Chillers and METASYS BAS Controls. The York Chillers and the METASYS BAS Controls work together as one component and only qualified York/BAS technicians can operate and repair the METASYS BAS Controls. Without Johnson Controls servicing the METASYS BAS Controls, the York Chillers would not operate. Johnson Controls is the only vendor that can provide the updates to the METASYS BAS Controls, no other vendor has rights to the software. Johnson Controls is the only known vendor that has the technicians allowed to service York/BAS units and systems. Johnson Controls of Memphis, TN is the only dealer for this area represented for York.

The Government intends to award a firm fixed price Purchase Order on or about November 13, 2019, using Simplified Acquisition Procedures. This Notice of lntent is not considered a Request for Quotation (R

Description:
The requirement is for repairs to the A-10 Digital Video Audio Data Recorder (DVADR) and the Removable Mass Memory Device (RMMD).
This solicitation will result in a single-award, five-year ordering period plus three option year Requirements Contract. It is the intention of the Air Force to award this as a sole source requirement to Zodiac Data Systems due to lack of Data.
NSNs: 5836-01-526-2746 (DVADR) and 5836-01-615-0941 FJ (RMMD)
Source code for the DVADR and RMMD are required knowledge for this repair.
Manufacturer: Zodiac Data Systems, Cage: 1UAE7
Form, Fit, Function:

DVADR
-8" X 8" X 8" Weight: 8 lbs
-Black case containing circuit card assemblies and removable media memory.
-Provides a digital video recording of right and left MFCD, HUD, and CDU video data, as well as audio from the aircraft intercom.

RMMD
-5.3543" X 3.2283" X 1.0236" weight: 1.10 lbs
-Metal and two circuit card assemblies
-Records flight Video/Sudio and 1553 bus traffic. Map overlays, CADRG maps, JP2 map images, etc

<DESC>The Federal Correctional Institution, Sandstone, Minnesota intends to issue solicitation 15B41320Q00000004 for the 2nd Quarter, FY 2020 milk requirement. This solicitation is 100% set-aside for small business under NAICS 311511. The Government contemplates award of a firm-fixed price type contract from this solicitation. The Government reserves the right upon award to make formal single or multiple awards. The Government will award to the responsible offeror(s), whose offer conforming to the solicitation, is considered to be most advantageous to the Government based on best value. Pricing will be FOB destination. The solicitation will be available on or about November 5, 2019 and will be distributed solely through the General Services Administration's Federal Business Opportunities web site at http://www.fbo.gov. The site provides downloading instructions. Hard copies of the solicitation will not be distributed. All future information regarding this acquisition, including amendments, will be distributed through this site. Interested parties are responsible for monitoring this site to ensure they have the most up-to-date information about this solicitation. The Government may make awards without discussions; therefore, offerors are instructed to submit their best offer initially. Faith-Based and Community-Based Organizations have the right to submit quotes equally with other organizations for contracts for which they are eligible. All contractors doing business with the Federal Government must be registered in the System for Award Management (SAM) database at

1102 19 Department of the Navy Naval Facilities Engineering Command NAVFAC Mid-Atlantic, ROICC Camp Lejeune 28547-2521 Z 561790 Department of the Navy, Naval Facilities Engineering Command, Mid-Atlantic 1005 Michael Road, Room 20 Camp Lejeune NC 28547-2521 Winter Pool Maintenance N40085-20-Q-4013 053120 06292020 Regenia D. Guest, Contract Specialist, Phone 91045125825223, Email regenia.d.guest@navy.mil - Ryan McRacken, Contract Specialist, Phone 910-451-2582 Ext. 5225, Fax 910-451-5629, Email ryan.mcracken@navy.mil The contractor will be required to clean and winterize the pools at the end of the pool season, clean and prepare each pool for the beginning of the normal pool season.  Types of work include, but are not limited to: pressure washing to clean the pools, removing items into storage, installing pool covers, adjusting pH levels, shocking the pools, cleaning and removing debris from covers and surrounding pool areas, maintaining water level, and operating robotic crawlers.

The Government plans on awarding a FFP contract with a base period of one (1) year plus four (4) option years as a result of the solicitation.  The total term of the contract, including all options, will not exceed 60 months.  https://www.fbo.gov/spg/DON/NAVFAC/N62470CL/N40085-20-Q-4013/listing.html Link To Document Total Small Business US 28456 Marine Corps Base, Camp Lejeune Jacksonville, NC 1101 19 Department of the Navy Naval Supply Systems Command NAVSUP Weapon Systems Support Mechanicsburg PA

Hammerle, Contracting Specialist, Phone 3146377447, Email thomas.w.hammerle.civ@mail.mil TITLE: CHAPEL MUSIC SUPPORT SERVICES, CASERMA EDERLE, VICENZA, ITALY

The Regional Contracting Office for the 414th CSB in Vicenza, Italy will use the responses to this notice to determine potential responsible vendors capable of providing the services described herein.

DESCRIPTION OF THE WORK: The Contractor shall provide musical accompaniment (organ and piano) for Catholic and Protestant worship services, rehearsals and special worship services in support of the USAG-Italy Religious Support Office.

PLACE OF PERFORMANCE: Caserma Ederle, Vicenza Italy.

SERVICE PERFORMANCE: The Contractor shall provide musical accompaniment service in support of Catholic and Protestant worship services each Sunday. Additionally The Contractor shall provide musical accompaniment in support of special services. (i.e. Memorial Services, Holy Days, Midnight Mass, Easter Sunrise, Funerals, etc.)
PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE: The Government anticipates awarding a contract with a period of performance starting 3 February 2020 to 2 February 2024.

TYPE OF THE CONTRACT: Firm Fixed Price

WHO CAN PARTICIPATE: Quoters that possses a Chamber of Commerce Certificate of Membership (Certificato Storico di Iscrizione alla Camera di Commercio), NOT older than 90 days at the time set forth for the receipt of offers. Self-certifications are NOT allowed and will be rejected. The business purpose (oggetto sociale) should include but is not limited to: music support services (This requirement is only applicable to Italian companies)

0614 19 Nuclear Regulatory Commission Acquisition Management Division Acquisition Management Division 20852-2738 D 541519 11555 Rockville Pike Rockville MD 20852-2738 Support Services for the NRC Technical Training Center Simulator Configuration Control Program 31310019R0039 073119 Jerry Purcell , Contract Specialist, Email jerry.purcell@nrc.gov - Heriberto Colon Jr., Contracting Officer, Email Heriberto.Colon@nrc.gov

The objective of this acquisition is to obtain support services for the TTC simulator configuration control program which enables the NRC training program for inspectors, license examiners, and others which, in turn, supports the NRC's mission to provide reasonable assurance of adequate protection of public health and safety, to promote the common defense and security, and to protect the environment.

The goal is to acquire simulator configuration control program support for all simulators currently located at the TTC or that may be installed at the TTC during the term of this contract. The support will consist of hardware support, software support, general support, and simulator engineering expert support.

Hardware support shall include various hardware preventative maintenance, corrective maintenance, and modification activities. The activities will include troubleshooting, testing, component installation, vendor support, hardware modifications, and hardware configuration management including the use of an administrative configuration control system.

Software support shall include network administration, software preventative maintenance, and software modification activities. The activities will include issue identification, troubleshooting, modifications, imaging, backups, testing, updates, network administration, and software configuration control including the use of an administrative configuration control s

The solicitation may include various small business set asides pursuant to the Small Business Act (15 U.S.C. 631, et seq.). This may include partial or total small business set-asides, service-disabled veteran-owned (SDVO) set-asides, quantities reserved for 8(a) and AbilityOne firms, and price evaluation preferences for qualified HUBZone small businesses. Quantities reserved for 8(a) and SDVO firms where only one of those firms meets AMS qualification requirements will be awarded on a sole source basis through issuance of a letter RFP issued directly to those companies.


The solicitation is expected to be available on or after October 30, 2019, and will be available electronically through USDA Web Based Supply Chain Management (WBSCM) system located at https://portal.wbscm.usda.gov/publicprocurement and also through a link to the aforementioned website in the document package attached to the solicitation notice on the Federal Business Opportunities website. Travis, Contract Specialist, Phone 499641835108, Email jason.w.travis.civ@mail.mil The U.S. Army, 928th Contracting Battalion (CBN) / Regional Contracting Office (RCO) Bavaria, Unit 28130, APO, AE 09227, intends to issue a solicitation to award a commercial, Single Award, Firm-Fixed Price (FFP), Indefinite Delivery Type (IDT) contract for the U.S. Army Garrison Bavaria. This requirement will include all refuse collection for both Tower Barracks-Grafenwoehr and Rose Barracks-Vilseck. The Period of Performance is expected to start on 01MAY 2020 and includes a one (1) year Base Period with four (4) one-year Option Periods IAW FAR Clause 52.217-9. The Product Service Code (PSC) code for this requirement is S205 (Trash/Garbage Collection). The Government anticipates releasing the solicitation under full and open competition before the end of calendar year 2019, but the Government reserves the right to delay or cancel the solicitation at any time prior to award. 0613 19 Nuclear Regulatory Commission Acquisition Management Division Acquisition Management Division 20852-2738 R 541519 11555 Rockville Pike Rockville MD 20852-2738 SUPPORT SERVICES FOR THE NRC TECHNICAL TRAINING CENTER SIMULATOR CONFIGURATION CONTROL PROGRAM OCHCO-19-0041 071219 Jerry Purcell , Contract Specialist, Phone 3014157142, Email jerry.purcell@nrc.gov - Heriberto Colon Jr., Contracting Officer, Email Heriberto.Colon@nrc.gov The U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) has a requirement for a Nuclear Power Plant Simulator for its Technical Training Center (TTC) in Chattanooga, TN.

The solicitation and resultant contract shall be issued in accordance with FAR Subpart 13.5. The resultant purchase order will consist of a base period of one year (beginning on or about September 16, 2019) plus four one-year options to extend the term of the purchase order.

The government intends to award a purchase order resulting from the solicitation to the responsible quoter whose quotation represents the best value to the government, evaluated cost/price and other factors considered. Anticipated evaluation factors for award include adequacy of technical approach; past performance information; adequacy of management and staffing plan; adequacy of corporate capability and experience, and Evaluated Cost/Price.

The NRC uses Compusearch Software Systems' secure and auditable two-way web portal, FedConnect, to communicate with offerors and contractors. FedConnect provides bi-directional communication between the offeror/contractor and the NRC throughout the preaward, award and postaward phases. Therefore, in order submit a proposal, the offeror must register to use FedConnect at https://www.fedconnect.net/FedConnect/PUblicUserRegistration.aspx.
Assistance with FedConnect is provided by Compusearch Software Systems, not the NRC. FedCo

The NAICS code assigned to this procurement will be 561320 with a size standard of $30.0 million. The VA will award a firm, fixed price, commercial services contract. Contract use will be limited to Minneapolis VA Health Care System only. The solicitation will contain requirements for a 12-month base period contract, with four (4) 12-month option periods to renew. Service will begin April 1, 2020 through March 31, 2021 for the base period. The solicitation is scheduled to be issued on or about October 25, 2019 and will be issued via FedBizOpps Electronic Posting System at http://www.fedbizopps.gov. The contractor shall furnish three (3) opticians to provide onsite optician services to eligible beneficiaries of the Department of Veterans Affairs Medical Center, Minneapolis VA Health Care System (MVAHCS). The Minneapolis VA Health Care System (MVAHCS) has a requirement for the provision of services of three (3) Opticians to dispense and fit eyeglasses and frames to Veterans. Services are required by the Minneapolis VA Health Care System (VAHCS), Surgical/Specialty Care Patient Service Line (SSC PSL) in support of the Ophthalmology Department. The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality (AHRQ) of the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services will be soliciting proposals for Annual Updates and Technical Support of AHRQ Quality Indicators for Public Use (QIs) V.
The Center for Quality Improvement and Patient Safety (CQuIPS) within the Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality (AHRQ) of the United States Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS) requires the annual updating and technical support of the AHRQ Quality IndicatorsTM (QIs) (2019-2024).
The AHRQ QI Annual Update and Technical Support and Contract shall focus solely on the operational aspects of updating the QI software and supporting the users of AHRQ QIs who are focused on quality improvements by providing updated software,

Neeb, Contract Specialist, Phone 2103958720, Email robert.neeb.2@us.af.mil - Alicia Kooda, Contract Specialist, Phone 2103958675, Email alicia.kooda@us.af.mil THIS IS A PRE‐SOLICITATION NOTICE FOR PLANNING PURPOSES ONLY AND INCLUDES DRAFT REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL INFORMATION.

PRE‐SOLICITATION NOTICE:

THIS IS NOT A FORMAL SOLICITATION FOR A REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL (RFP). PLEASE DO NOT SUBMIT A PROPOSAL.

This Pre‐Solicitation Notice is being issued for the purpose of advising potential Offerors of the Government's requirement for follow‐on contracts to current Base Realignment and Closure (BRAC) environmental remediation activities at 40 BRAC installations. This new effort will effectively be called the BRAC Environmental Construction Optimization Services (BECOS). The subject pre‐solicitation notice is to provide information for early planning and does not constitute a commitment, implied or otherwise, that a procurement action will be issued. There is no entitlement to payment by the Government of direct or indirect costs or charges that arise as a result of the provision
of this information. Note that official information concerning this pre‐solicitation will be provided by the Contracting Officer only.

GENERAL INFORMATION: The 772d Enterprise Sourcing Squadron/Environmental Services (772 ESS/PKS) in conjunction with the Air Force Civil Engineer Center (AFCEC), at Joint Base San Antonio‐Lackland, TX, is contemplating a performance‐based approach for follow‐on contracts to current Base Realignment and Closure (BRAC) environmental remediation activities at 40 BRAC installati

Roberts, Contracting Officer, Phone 575-572-5293, Email sally.roberts@us.af.mil This presolicitation notice is an announcement of a requirement for Vertical Transportation Equipment Maintenance and Repairs as defined in the Performance Work Statement (PWS) at Holloman AFB NM 88330.

DESCRIPTION OF WORK: The Contractor shall provide all management, tools, supplies, equipment, parts and labor necessary to maintain, repair, and inspect the real property vertical transportation equipment (VTE) at Holloman Air Force Base in a manner that will ensure continuous and safe operation (full maintenance and repair). This full maintenance contract signifies a VTE service provider (Contractor) takes total service responsibility for the equipment identified in the contract. This includes inspections, maintenance, repairs, replacements, and routine and emergency service calls. This contract allows the Government to budget total yearly costs and eliminates concerns relating to individual parts repair or replacement invoicing. The Contractor assumes all responsibility and determines the amount of service visits and types of service required to keep the VTE operating safely and within the performance objectives of the PWS.

Work conducted on VTE will be in accordance with the American Society of Mechanical Engineers (ASME) A17.1-2007 (or applicable code for year VTE put in service), Safety Code for Elevators and Escalators (ASME A17.1), ANSI/ASME A17.2-2010, Guide for Inspection of Elevators, Escalators, and Moving Walks (app

This notice of intent to award a sole source contract is not a request for competitive proposals. However, in accordance with FAR 5.207 (c) (16) (ii), all responsible sources that feel they can meet the Government s requirements detailed above may submit a capability statement which shall be considered by the agency. All offerors must have an active registration in the System for Award Management (SAM) www.sam.gov. General Information:

Solicitation No. FA3030-20-R-0001

Classification Code: Z - Maintenance, repair, and alteration of real property

Subject:

This synopsis is to establish a new Simplified Acquisition of Base Engineering Requirements (SABER) Indefinite Delivery/Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract at Goodfellow AFB, TX located near San Angelo, TX.  The contractor shall furnish all plant, labor, equipment, materials, transportation, services, and incidentals necessary to perform all work involved in said contract. Work will consist of a broad range of maintenance, alterations, repair and minor construction work on real property at Goodfellow AFB and the Goodfellow AFB Recreation Camp.  Individual, firm-fixed price task orders will be issued on an as needed basis using the unit price book (Facilities Construction Cost with RSMeans data by Gordian).The contractor shall perform functions specified in the contract task orders for the unit price sum specified in the unit price book multiplied by the appropriate coefficient.  This solicitation is set-aside for certified 8(a) business concerns located in the state of Texas.  The NAICS Code for this solicitation is

STE. 506;St. Paul MN 55101 Q--Perfusionists Services 36C26320R0008 01232020 Daniel Vagts daniel.vagts@va.gov Daniel Vagts This is PRE-SOLICITATION NOTICE The US Department of Veteran Affairs Medical Center, Minneapolis, MN, hereinafter referred to as MVAHCS located at One Veterans Drive, Minneapolis, MN 55417. The NAICS code assigned to this procurement will be 621111 with a size standard of $15 million. The VA will award a firm, fixed price, commercial services contract. Contract use will be limited to Minneapolis VA Health Care System only. The solicitation will contain requirements for a 12-month base period contract, with four (4) 12-month option periods to renew. Service will begin May 1, 2020 through April 30, 2021 for the base period. The solicitation is scheduled to be issued on or about December 1, 2019 and will be issued via FedBizOpps Electronic Posting System at http://www.fedbizopps.gov. Services Provided: The contractor shall provide the Minneapolis VA Health Care System (MVAHCS) contractual services for an American Board of Cardiovascular Perfusionists to provide clinical perfusion services coverage to eligible veteran beneficiaries of the MVAHCS. Perfusionist services are required on-site daily, Monday through Friday; On-call coverage is required. One key personnel are required to be onsite 52 weeks per year, Monday Friday; excluding federal holidays.
